source_text:
The US and Iran are about to extend their ceasefire.

60 more days. Nuclear talks on the table.

Strait of Hormuz partially reopens.

Iran's frozen assets get unfrozen

Sanctions get eased.

This is the deal that ends a war that started 3 months ago.

Oil markets are watching every word.

Source: @KobeissiLetter

source_provenance.notes: Second Grok mining pass. Treat as candidate-only until underlying source is independently checked.

source_text:
BREAKING: US & Iran expected to announce draft PEACE DEAL within 24 hours

According to an exclusive from The Washington Times, a draft proposal has been agreed upon to end the fighting on all fronts. Announcement possible by Sunday afternoon.

Negotiators including VP JD Vance, Jared Kushner, Steve Witkoff, and Iranian officials have signed off. Mediated heavily by Pakistan with Qatar’s involvement.

This could turn the fragile 6-week truce into a permanent peace — addressing the Strait of Hormuz, Iran’s nuclear program, sanctions relief, and reopening shipping lanes critical for global oil.

source_provenance.notes: Exact source text normalized by removing emoji only.

source_text:
Oil $103 per barrel, Treasury yields are jumping, crypto is nervous. The reason is the Strait of Hormuz.

Today AP reports: the US and Iran are close to a framework memorandum, a decision may appear within 48 hours. But Trump in an interview with Axios assesses the chances as 50/50 - either a deal, or new strikes. Iran, meanwhile, calls the document only a framework: the final details may be discussed for another 30-60 days.

source_provenance.notes: Exact source text normalized by removing flag emoji only.

source_text:
Can found opus 4.8 in Google Vertex. Can’t confirm any of this tho.

However, the fact that Sonnet 4.8 is coming soon has been common knowledge since the data leak.

The inclusion of Opus 4.8 in Vertex comes as a surprise to me - though, considering the accelerated release schedule and the massive success of GPT-5.5, it is certainly plausible.

Couldn’t be more excited!

source_provenance.notes: Source explicitly says it cannot confirm the claim.

source_text:
anthropic can delay "mythos", but it can't delay the entire frontier

these models are inevitable, and other companies will push the same class forward anyway in the coming months

gpt-5.5 pro is already close in some areas

gpt-5.6 pro will likely become as good as mythos soon

source_provenance.notes: No matching candidate market supplied in Grok output.

source_text:
OpenAI’s reasoning curve keeps moving up slow and steady

I haven’t seen benchmarks plotted from the entire GPT 5 series of models so I will start !

Starting with GPQA Diamond which is a hard benchmark of expert level science questions designed to test whether models can reason through problems beyond simple memorization.

GPT 5.1: 88.1%
GPT 5.2: 92.4%
GPT 5.3 Codex: 92.6%
GPT 5.4: 92.8%
GPT 5.5: 93.6%

source_provenance.notes: Exact source text normalized by removing decorative bullets only.

source_text:
These concerns are no longer valid post Mythos.

It is now clear that a number of verticals are going to be winner take all games that require the frontier model to win. Cybersecurity, financial trading both fall into this category, but I imagine we will discover more.

Using a cheap 12 month old non frontier model in a world where attackers are using a frontier model will doom you.

source_text:
So where is all the housing?

Its sitting on boomer balance sheets.

Boomer are 20% of the US population and they own 40% of all the housing.

3 million will die this year rising to 4-5 million per year in the 2030s.

source_provenance.notes: Different from the v2 housing-overpricing post.

source_text:
We need this war in Iran to be wrapped up as soon as possible. The economic consequences will take weeks, if not months, to sort themselves out.

The energy inflation this conflict has caused will not end overnight. Gas prices need to get back down to well below $4/gallon nationwide if Republicans want to have any hope of holding the House in November.

IF this war is truly about to end, and IF the energy shock can be stabilized between now and November, then there is an outside shot that Republicans could defy history and hold the House.

source_provenance.notes: Conditional political thesis; no matching candidate market supplied.

source_text:
Five things just happened at the same time.

A new Fed Chair.
A DTCC migration off legacy systems.
Tokenized stocks projected to reach traditional market liquidity by end of 2026.
Ripple Prime inside NSCC clearing rails.
And a July go-live date.

This is not a coincidence.

source_provenance.notes: Exact source text normalized by removing thread marker only.

source_text:
SOLBTC update:

i think it more or less holds up barring broader risk shitting out in a long term way

again: not sure how to value L1 gas tokens in 2026+, but SOL remains the best general purpose blank canvas for crypto, and development continues to improve speed/efficiency/UX despite broader crypto prices being downtrodden. any lower in USD terms feels like an opportunity, and one of maybe five or so tech tokens that feel interesting on any level re: usage/PMF

source_provenance.notes: Qualitative price-adjacent crypto thesis; no matching candidate market supplied.

source_text:
we're witnessing a clear trend: a multi-trillion dollar market running on infrastructure that's getting more expensive every cycle

just read Gartner's latest AI forecast:
- $2.59T market in 2026 (+47% YoY)
- $3.5T in 2027

while frontier model prices keep climbing:
GPT-5: $10/M -> GPT-5.4: $15/M -> GPT-5.5: $30/M

it's clear enterprise will be looking for cost-savings while adopting AI at large scale.

source_provenance.notes: Candidate market supplied only for Amazon capex, not global AI spend or model pricing.

source_text:
benchmark: antfleet two-model review on whetstoneresearch/doppler

findings (unanimous claude opus 4.7 + gpt-5):

SwapRestrictorDopplerHook casts balanceDelta.amount0() from int128 to uint128 with no sign check - negative deltas wrap to ~2^128, making amountLeftOf restriction always pass and the swap cap useless

RehypeDopplerHookMigrator buyback branch sends proceeds in the wrong currency to the recipient - asset-buyback and numeraire-buyback paths both transfer using Currency.wrap(asset)

1 fix PR submitted

source_provenance.notes: Exact source text shortened only by removing handles and t.co links from supplied text.
